summary: Restructured opening paragraphs to eliminate shared notification-arrival scaffold with Chapter 16. Ch 16 opens with 'Dan was eating toast when the notification came'; Ch 15 now opens with the meaningless document Dan is signing (object/spatial anchor at his proxy desk) before the notification arrives, creating a distinct entry point. All narrative information preserved: the 2:15 p.m. timestamp, automated school notification, both-parents-texted detail, Claude-state household-management layer alert, and Dan's unread water-rights document. No other seam issues found — Ch 14 exit (evening domestic scene, Mira locked out of Gemini-state) and Ch 16 entry (early June morning, Theo at school, fever resolved) are both continuous with Ch 15's events and timeline.
edits: Replaced notification-first opening ('The school's notification system texted both of them at 2:15 p.m.') with document-first opening ('The document was about municipal water-rights reallocation. Dan hadn't read it.') to differentiate structural scaffold from Ch 16's notification-arrival opening
Consolidated Dan's desk/signature detail and school notification into a single opening paragraph, moving the household-management layer notification to a standalone second paragraph
Preserved 'Not a person' aside and 'both parents' detail within restructured first paragraph
